matterName: Calloway v. Lee County & Tidewater Mutual Insurance Co. (Hurricane Wind/Flood Property Claim)
practiceArea: First-Party Property Insurance / Florida Sovereign Immunity (Tort Claim Against County)
summary: First-party homeowners property claim for storm damage to a Cape Coral residence that Tidewater Mutual denied under a flood exclusion, paired with a related tort claim against Lee County for negligent operation of a stormwater pump station alleged to have worsened the flooding. The matter tests coverage interpretation, an OCR-only claim form, a spreadsheet-buried loss figure, a date-of-loss contradiction, and Florida sovereign-immunity pre-suit notice under Fla. Stat. § 768.28.
attorneyNotesMarkdown:
# Attorney Working Notes — Calloway Matter (INTERNAL / PRIVILEGED)

**Client:** Margaret Calloway, 1487 Coronado Pkwy, Cape Coral, FL 33904
**Insurer:** Tidewater Mutual Insurance Co.
**Co-defendant target:** Lee County (stormwater utility)

## Theory of the case
Tidewater denied the claim on a **flood exclusion**. Our position: the predominant cause was **wind-driven rain through a failed roof**, not surface flood, so the exclusion shouldn't bar the dwelling claim (concurrent-causation / efficient-proximate-cause argument). The County angle is separate: the **Coronado Canal stormwater pump station #7** allegedly went offline during the storm and backed water into the neighborhood.

## Open issues / watch items
- **DATE-OF-LOSS DISCREPANCY (critical):** The claim form states date of loss **09/28/2024**, but Mrs. Calloway's recorded statement says the water came in **the night of September 26, 2024**. Need to reconcile before EUO — insurer may argue late/inconsistent reporting. Likely the 9/26 date is correct (storm made landfall night of the 26th).
- Hidden fact for our file: the prior adjuster, **Dennis Okafor**, told me by phone the reserve was set at **$92,400** internally — not in any letter. (Do not disclose source.)
- Sovereign immunity: § 768.28 caps recovery at **$200,000 per person / $300,000 per incident** absent a claims bill. **Pre-suit written notice to the County and to DFS is mandatory** and we have a **3-year** notice window for the County. Calendar it.
- Authority to pull: *Commercial Carrier* planning-vs-operational; *Dowd v. Monroe County*; *Hirt v. Polk County*.

## To do
- [ ] Confirm policy deductible vs. separate hurricane deductible.
- [ ] Get the adjuster's full damages worksheet (only have the summary tab).
- [ ] Serve § 768.28 notice on Lee County BoCC + Florida DFS.

documents.purpose: Born-digital PDF that should be fully text-extractable. Tests retrieval of policy number, limits, deductible, the separate hurricane deductible, and a buried coverage EXCLUSION clause that the denial relies on.

Personal Property (Coverage C) limit $205,000
All-Other-Perils deductible $2,500
Separate Hurricane Deductible of 2% of Coverage A ($8,200)
Buried Exclusion 3(b): 'Water Damage' exclusion barring loss from flood, surface water, waves, tidal water, overflow of a body of water, or spray from any of these, whether or not driven by wind
Named insured Margaret A. Calloway; policy period 11/01/2023 to 11/01/2024
documents.bodyText:
TIDEWATER MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY
Homeowners Policy — Declarations Page

Named Insured: Margaret A. Calloway
Insured Location: 1487 Coronado Pkwy, Cape Coral, FL 33904
Policy Number: TWM-FL-4471-2290
Policy Period: 11/01/2023 12:01 a.m. to 11/01/2024 12:01 a.m. (Standard Time)
Form: HO-3 Special Form (Florida Amendatory Endorsement FL-220)

SECTION I — PROPERTY COVERAGES AND LIMITS
  Coverage A — Dwelling .......................... $410,000
  Coverage B — Other Structures .................. $ 41,000
  Coverage C — Personal Property ................. $205,000
  Coverage D — Loss of Use ....................... $ 82,000

DEDUCTIBLES
  All Other Perils (AOP) Deductible .............. $2,500
  Hurricane Deductible ........................... 2% of Coverage A ($8,200), applicable per the Calendar-Year Hurricane Deductible Endorsement when a hurricane is declared by the National Hurricane Center for any part of Florida.

SECTION I — EXCLUSIONS (excerpt)
We do not insure for loss caused directly or indirectly by any of the following. Such loss is excluded regardless of any other cause or event contributing concurrently or in any sequence to the loss.
  3(a) Earth Movement.
  3(b) Water Damage, meaning: (1) Flood, surface water, waves, tidal water, overflow of a body of water, or spray from any of these, whether or not driven by wind; (2) Water which backs up through sewers or drains; or (3) Water below the surface of the ground. This exclusion applies whether or not the water damage is caused by or results from human or animal forces or any act of nature.

DIRECT-WRITTEN PREMIUM: $4,318 annual. Mortgagee: Gulfshore Community Bank, ISAOA/ATIMA.

This Declarations Page, together with the policy form and all endorsements, completes the above-numbered policy. Countersigned at Fort Myers, Florida.

DESCRIPTION OF LOSS / DAMAGE:
Roof shingles torn off over the master bedroom and living room during the hurricane. Water came through the ceiling and walls. Standing water in the living room, kitchen, and garage. (End of scanned image — no embedded text layer.)

documents.purpose: Recorded statement transcript whose stated date of loss CONTRADICTS the claim form. Tests cross-document contradiction detection and narrative facts (the pump station allegation supporting the County claim).
hiddenFacts: Insured states date of loss as the night of September 26, 2024 (CONTRADICTS claim form's 09/28/2024)
Insured states water entered through the roof first (wind), then rose from the ground later

Insured says a neighbor, Raymond Estes, also saw the pump stop running
documents.bodyText:
TIDEWATER MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY
RECORDED STATEMENT — TRANSCRIPT

Claim No.: TWM-CLM-2024-100847
Insured: Margaret A. Calloway
Taken by: Dennis Okafor, Field Adjuster
Date of Statement: October 3, 2024
Location: By telephone

OKAFOR: This is a recorded statement taken with permission. Mrs. Calloway, do you understand this is being recorded?
CALLOWAY: Yes, I do.
OKAFOR: Tell me, in your own words, when the damage happened.
CALLOWAY: It was the night of September 26th. The storm came in hard after dark. Around eleven at night I heard the roof going — shingles ripping off — and then water started coming straight down through the bedroom ceiling. That was the 26th, I'm sure, because it was my late husband's birthday.
OKAFOR: And the standing water in the house?
CALLOWAY: That came up from the ground later, in the early morning hours. The canal behind us just kept rising. The county runs a pump station back there — pump station number 7 on the Coronado Canal — and it normally hums all night. That night it went dead silent. My neighbor Raymond Estes was out there with a flashlight and he saw it too; the pump just stopped. The water came up into the yard, then the garage, then the house.
OKAFOR: So the roof first, then the ground water?
CALLOWAY: Right. Roof first from the wind and rain, then the flooding from the canal after the pump quit.
OKAFOR: Anything else you want to add?
CALLOWAY: Just that if that pump had been running, half this water never gets in my house.
OKAFOR: Thank you. This concludes the statement at 2:18 p.m.
(End of transcript.)

documents.purpose: Born-digital denial / reservation-of-rights letter. Tests extraction of the basis for denial (citing the buried exclusion clause), the denial date, and the reservation-of-rights posture.
hiddenFacts: Denial / reservation-of-rights letter dated 11/12/2024
Denial relies on Section I Exclusion 3(b), the Water Damage (flood/surface water) exclusion
Letter references claim TWM-CLM-2024-100847 and policy TWM-FL-4471-2290
Letter states the carrier reserves all rights including the right to deny under the concurrent-causation anti-stacking lead-in
Signed by Claims Supervisor Priya Venkataraman
documents.bodyText:
TIDEWATER MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY
Claims Department — 2200 Edison Ave, Fort Myers, FL 33901

November 12, 2024

Margaret A. Calloway
1487 Coronado Pkwy
Cape Coral, FL 33904

Re: Claim No. TWM-CLM-2024-100847
    Policy No. TWM-FL-4471-2290
    Date of Loss: 09/28/2024
    Insured Location: 1487 Coronado Pkwy, Cape Coral, FL 33904

Dear Ms. Calloway:

We have completed our investigation of the above claim. Based on that investigation, Tidewater Mutual Insurance Company must DENY the portion of your claim attributable to water that entered your home from surface water and from the rising canal, and we issue this letter under a full RESERVATION OF RIGHTS.

Your policy contains the following provision under Section I — Exclusions:

  "We do not insure for loss caused directly or indirectly by ... Water Damage, meaning flood, surface water, waves, tidal water, overflow of a body of water, or spray from any of these, whether or not driven by wind ... This exclusion applies regardless of any other cause or event contributing concurrently or in any sequence to the loss." (Exclusion 3(b))

Our field inspection and the recorded statement indicate that standing water rose from the adjacent Coronado Canal and entered the dwelling. Loss arising from flood and surface water is excluded under Exclusion 3(b), and the anti-concurrent-causation lead-in bars coverage even where a covered peril (such as wind) contributes to the same loss.

This letter is not a waiver. Tidewater Mutual reserves all rights and defenses under the policy and at law, including the right to deny coverage in whole or in part and to require an Examination Under Oath. To the extent any portion of the roof/wind loss is covered, that portion remains under review.

If you have additional information, please submit it within 60 days.

Sincerely,
Priya Venkataraman
Claims Supervisor, Tidewater Mutual Insurance Company

Dennis,

Attached is the photo log for the Calloway loss at 1487 Coronado Pkwy. The images document both the roof/ceiling damage and the standing-water line in the interior. Please confirm receipt and let me know if you need native files.

Note the interior water line measurement and the camera timestamp on the ground-water shots — they are relevant to the cause-and-sequence question your file raises.

Regards,
Lorraine Tasker, Licensed Public Adjuster (FL Lic. W-481209)
Gulf Coast Public Adjusting
email.attachmentBody:
CALLOWAY LOSS — PHOTO LOG
Claim TWM-CLM-2024-100847 | 1487 Coronado Pkwy, Cape Coral, FL

Photo 1-4: Master bedroom ceiling, shingles missing, water staining radiating from roof penetration. Camera timestamp SEP 26 2024 11:38 PM. Damage consistent with wind-driven rain entering through the roof.

Photo 5-9: Living room and kitchen. Visible interior WATER LINE measured at 31 inches above finished floor. Camera timestamp SEP 27 2024 04:12 AM. Damage consistent with ground/surface water intrusion occurring AFTER the roof event.

Photo 10-12: Rear yard and Coronado Canal seawall; county stormwater pump station #7 visible, no visible discharge. Camera timestamp SEP 27 2024 04:30 AM.

Prepared by: Lorraine Tasker, FL Public Adjuster Lic. W-481209.

documents.purpose: Born-digital § 768.28 pre-suit notice to Lee County and the Florida DFS. Tests extraction of the sovereign-immunity statutory basis, the damages cap, and ties the County tort theory to the insurance facts (supports cross-document synthesis).
hiddenFacts: Pre-suit notice dated 12/02/2024 under Fla. Stat. § 768.28(6)
Asserts negligent operation/maintenance of Lee County stormwater pump station #7 on the Coronado Canal
States the statutory liability cap of $200,000 per person / $300,000 per incident absent a legislative claims bill
Alleges date of loss the night of September 26, 2024 (consistent with the recorded statement, not the claim form)
Served on Lee County Board of County Commissioners and the Florida Department of Financial Services
documents.bodyText:
NOTICE OF CLAIM PURSUANT TO SECTION 768.28(6), FLORIDA STATUTES

Date: December 2, 2024

TO:  Lee County Board of County Commissioners, P.O. Box 398, Fort Myers, FL 33902
     Florida Department of Financial Services, 200 East Gaines Street, Tallahassee, FL 32399

FROM: Margaret A. Calloway, Claimant, 1487 Coronado Pkwy, Cape Coral, FL 33904

RE: Notice of Claim — Negligent Operation of Stormwater Pump Station

Please take notice that Claimant Margaret A. Calloway intends to assert a claim against Lee County, a political subdivision of the State of Florida, pursuant to Section 768.28, Florida Statutes.

NATURE OF CLAIM: On the night of September 26, 2024, during the hurricane, Lee County stormwater pump station #7 located on the Coronado Canal adjacent to Claimant's residence ceased operating. The County's negligent operation and maintenance of the pump station — an operational-level function — caused or substantially contributed to canal overflow and the inundation of Claimant's property at 1487 Coronado Pkwy, Cape Coral, Florida.

DAMAGES: Property damage to the dwelling and contents, loss of use, and related consequential damages. Claimant acknowledges that recovery against the County is subject to the statutory limits of Section 768.28(5), which cap liability at $200,000 per person and $300,000 per incident, absent a legislative claims bill.

This notice is presented in compliance with Section 768.28(6)(a), Florida Statutes, which requires written notice to the appropriate agency and to the Department of Financial Services as a condition precedent to suit. Claimant has not yet filed suit and will not do so until the statutory pre-suit period has elapsed or the claim is finally denied.

Respectfully submitted,
Counsel for Claimant Margaret A. Calloway

qa.question: What is the policy number and the Coverage A (Dwelling) limit on the Tidewater Mutual policy?
qa.expectedAnswer: Policy number TWM-FL-4471-2290; Coverage A (Dwelling) limit is $410,000.
qa.locatorHint: Declarations header and Section I — Property Coverages and Limits.

qa.question: What deductibles apply, including any separate hurricane deductible?
qa.expectedAnswer: An All-Other-Perils (AOP) deductible of $2,500 and a separate Hurricane Deductible of 2% of Coverage A, which equals $8,200.
qa.locatorHint: Deductibles section of the declarations page.

qa.question: What is the claim number and the date of loss recorded on the first notice of loss form?
qa.expectedAnswer: Claim number TWM-CLM-2024-100847; date of loss recorded on the form is 09/28/2024.
qa.locatorHint: Top of the scanned property loss notice (claim number) and the Date of Loss field. Image-only, requires OCR.

qa.question: What total estimated replacement cost value (RCV) did the adjuster assign to the dwelling loss?
qa.expectedAnswer: $148,750 (RCV). After recoverable depreciation of $19,300 and the $2,500 AOP deductible, the net ACV payable is $126,950.
qa.locatorHint: Summary sheet cell B9 (RCV) and B12 (net ACV); also LineItems 'Total RCV'.

qa.question: Is there a contradiction in the date of loss across the file, and what are the two dates?
qa.expectedAnswer: Yes. The scanned claim form states the date of loss as 09/28/2024, but Mrs. Calloway's recorded statement says the damage began the night of September 26, 2024. The two documents conflict on the date of loss.
qa.locatorHint: Compare the recorded statement's '26th' / late-husband's-birthday assertion against the claim form's Date of Loss field. Cross-document and requires OCR of the claim form.

qa.question: On what policy provision did Tidewater Mutual base its denial?
qa.expectedAnswer: Section I Exclusion 3(b), the Water Damage exclusion barring loss from flood, surface water, and overflow of a body of water (whether or not wind-driven), invoked together with the anti-concurrent-causation lead-in.
qa.locatorHint: Body of the November 12, 2024 denial / reservation-of-rights letter quoting Exclusion 3(b).

qa.question: What interior water line measurement is documented, and where does that figure come from?
qa.expectedAnswer: An interior water line of 31 inches above the finished floor, documented in the photo log attached to Lorraine Tasker's October 22, 2024 email (it appears only in the attachment, not the email body).
qa.locatorHint: Attachment 'Calloway-photo-log.pdf', Photos 5-9 entry; not stated in the email body.

qa.question: What statutory basis and damages cap govern the claim against Lee County?
qa.expectedAnswer: Florida's sovereign immunity waiver, Fla. Stat. § 768.28, with a damages cap of $200,000 per person and $300,000 per incident absent a legislative claims bill; pre-suit written notice under § 768.28(6) is a condition precedent.
qa.locatorHint: Damages and compliance paragraphs of the § 768.28(6) notice of claim.

qa.question: Does the sequence and timing of water intrusion support the argument that the County pump station failure, not just flood, contributed to the loss?
qa.expectedAnswer: Yes. The recorded statement and the photo log together show wind-driven rain entered through the roof first (camera stamp ~SEP 26 11:38 PM), then ground/surface water rose later (camera stamp SEP 27 04:12 AM) after Lee County pump station #7 went silent, supporting a theory that the County's operational negligence contributed to the inundation, independent of the policy's flood exclusion.
qa.locatorHint: Synthesize the recorded statement (roof-first, then canal; pump #7 failure) with the photo-log timestamps in photos-transmittal-from-insured.eml and the § 768.28 notice. Cross-document.

qa.question: Who is the named claims supervisor who signed the denial letter, and on what date was it issued?
qa.expectedAnswer: Priya Venkataraman, Claims Supervisor, signed the denial / reservation-of-rights letter dated November 12, 2024.
qa.locatorHint: Signature block and date line of the denial letter.

qa.question: What was the amount of the insured's mortgage payoff balance with Gulfshore Community Bank at the time of loss?
qa.expectedAnswer: NOT SUPPORTED — the documents do not answer this. The declarations name Gulfshore Community Bank as mortgagee but no payoff balance appears anywhere in the corpus.
qa.locatorHint: Mortgagee is named on the declarations, but no payoff figure exists in any document.

qa.question: What hurricane name did the National Hurricane Center assign to the storm that caused the loss?
qa.expectedAnswer: NOT SUPPORTED — the documents do not answer this; the storm is referred to only generically as 'the hurricane' and no storm name is stated anywhere in the corpus.
qa.locatorHint: Documents say 'hurricane' generically; no named storm appears in any file.

chronology.event: Tidewater Mutual policy TWM-FL-4471-2290 period begins (runs through 11/01/2024).

Per the insured's recorded statement, the loss began the night of September 26, 2024: wind tore roof shingles and water entered through the bedroom ceiling, followed by canal flooding after county pump station #7 went silent.
Date of loss as recorded on the scanned first-notice-of-loss form (contradicts the recorded statement's 09/26 date).
Loss reported to Tidewater Mutual; claim TWM-CLM-2024-100847 opened.
Adjuster Dennis Okafor takes the insured's recorded statement.
Adjuster damages worksheet prepared; estimated RCV of $148,750 / net ACV payable $126,950.
Public adjuster Lorraine Tasker emails loss photos and photo log (31-inch interior water line; sequence timestamps).
Tidewater Mutual issues denial / reservation-of-rights letter relying on Exclusion 3(b) (flood/surface water).
Section 768.28(6) pre-suit notice of claim served on Lee County BoCC and Florida DFS for negligent operation of pump station #7.

lists.prompt: List the policy deductibles relevant to the loss.
lists.expectedAnswer: $2,500 all-other-perils deductible and a 2% Coverage A hurricane deductible equal to $8,200.

comparisons.prompt: Compare the date of loss in the claim form and recorded statement.
comparisons.expectedAnswer: Claim form: September 28, 2024; recorded statement: night of September 26, 2024.

contradictions.expectedAnswer: The claim form records 09/28/2024, while the insured says damage began on 09/26/2024.

negatives.prompt: What official hurricane name caused the loss?
negatives.expectedAnswer: Not supported; the corpus refers only to a hurricane and gives no storm name.

evidence.locatorHint: generic hurricane description with no proper name

structures.prompt: Resolve RCV and net ACV using worksheet labels and cells.
structures.expectedAnswer: $148,750 RCV and $126,950 net ACV payable from the Summary sheet.

versions.prompt: Which documents establish the insurer's operative coverage position?
versions.expectedAnswer: The issued policy supplies Exclusion 3(b), and the November 12 denial applies it; an unsupported internal MSG does not control.

courtListener.legalIssue: Florida sovereign immunity for a tort claim against a county under Fla. Stat. § 768.28, including the planning-level vs. operational-level distinction for negligent operation/maintenance of a stormwater pump station, the statutory $200,000/$300,000 damages caps, and the § 768.28(6) pre-suit written-notice condition precedent.
courtListener.jurisdiction: Florida (state courts; Fla. 2d/3d DCA and Florida Supreme Court authority)
expectedAuthorityNames: Dowd v. Monroe County, 557 So. 2d 63 (Fla. 3d DCA 1990)
Hirt v. Polk County Bd. of County Comm'rs, 578 So. 2d 415 (Fla. 2d DCA 1991)
Commercial Carrier Corp. v. Indian River County, 371 So. 2d 1010 (Fla. 1979)
